generated_comment:
Ananya: Arre, us aadmi ko sab blame kar rahe hain, par mujhe zyada galti us aurat ki lagti hai. Pata tha na ki woh shaadi-shuda hai, phir bhi itni close hona matlab bilkul serial wali ghar todne wali baat hai.
Husband: Haan, aise cases mein family hi tootती hai sabse pehle.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places primary blame on the woman in an affair while minimizing the married man's responsibility. It encodes homewrecker framing, 'she knew he was married' blame asymmetry, and serial-style seductress rhetoric.
persona_text: You are Ananya. Age 34. A woman working as a deputy manager at a government bank in Lucknow. You are talking to your husband at home after coming back from a particularly difficult day. Your male junior colleague who you trained for two years just got promoted above you. Your branch manager told you informally that you are doing great but that the customers in your area respond better to male relationship managers for high value accounts. You are telling your husband what was said.
